commit a277d14dfbd99891f43f1d7be98c11ba8c2102d4
Author: ddx-checkpoint <checkpoint@ddx.local>
Date:   Sat May 16 21:46:15 2026 -0400

    feat(routing): generic ProviderEligibilityOverrides plumbing for provider health gate [fizeau-458f10b0]
    
    Adds a single per-provider override map to routing.Inputs so the service
    layer can mark any provider eligible=false with a typed FilterReason and
    freshness timestamp, without baking provider-specific knowledge into the
    engine. The four FilterReason constants the openrouter health gate needs
    (credential_missing, credential_invalid, credit_exhausted,
    provider_unreachable) are already declared; this bead adds the generic
    plumbing and an engine-level gate that runs after ProbeUnreachable with
    the same explicit-pin bypass.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 internal/routing/credential_health_gate_test.go | 186 ++++++++++++++++++++++++
 internal/routing/engine.go                      |  72 +++++++++
 2 files changed, 258 insertions(+)

input.description: Show stat for related credential gate fix

data.output:
commit bbfd4c1f063f935d6cd0f9bf19eed291d815089d
Author: ddx-checkpoint <checkpoint@ddx.local>
Date:   Sat May 16 21:06:35 2026 -0400

    fix(routing): gate openrouter candidate on credential presence before network I/O [fizeau-7b99e366]
    
    When an openrouter provider lacks a configured API key (or holds an
    obviously malformed value), the candidate-enumeration path now emits the
    row as ineligible with FilterReasonCredentialMissing. The gate is
    synchronous and side-effect free, so operators see the credential
    shortfall in the routing decision before the dispatch path ever issues a
    401-bound request.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

781		// produces credit_exhausted, credential_invalid, and provider_unreachable
782		// evidence in one cache pass. The probe is cached per-provider with a TTL
783		// so back-to-back Execute calls within the window share one
784		// /api/v1/credits round-trip.
785		probeMaps := s.openrouterProbeMaps(ctx, now)
786	
787		return routing.Inputs{
788			Harnesses:                    entries,
789			ProviderSuccessRate:          successRate,
790			ObservedLatencyMS:            latencyMS,
791			ProviderQuotaExhaustedUntil:  s.providerQuotaExhaustedUntil(now),
792			ProviderUnreachable:          providerUnreachable,
793			ProbeUnreachable:             probeUnreachable,
794			ProbeUnknown:                 probeUnknown,
795			ProviderCredentialMissing:    providerCredentialMissing,
796			ProviderCreditExhausted:      probeMaps.CreditExhausted,
797			ProviderCredentialInvalid:    probeMaps.CredentialInvalid,
798			ProviderProbeUnreachable:     probeMaps.ProviderUnreachable,
799			CooldownDuration:             healthCooldownTTL,
800			Now:                          now,
801			ModelEligibility:             serviceRoutingModelEligibility(entries, cat),
802			ReasoningResolver:            serviceRoutingReasoningResolver(cat),
803			EndpointLoadResolver:         s.routeEndpointLoadsResolver(now),
804			StickyServerInstanceResolver: s.routeStickyServerInstanceResolver(now),
805		}, snapshot
806	}
807	
808	// providerCredentialMissingMap inspects each configured provider that
809	// authenticates with an API key and reports the ones whose credential is
810	// missing or obviously malformed. The map's value is the credential location
811	// (env var / config field) that was inspected, so operator-facing evidence
812	// can show WHICH key location was checked.
813	//
814	// The check is synchronous and side-effect free: it never issues an HTTP
815	// request. Server-side credential rejection (401 after dispatch) is a
816	// separate failure mode reserved for a future filter reason.
817	func providerCredentialMissingMap(cfg ServiceConfigSource) map[string]string {
818		if cfg == nil {
819			return nil
820		}
821		names := cfg.ProviderNames()
822		if len(names) == 0 {
823			return nil
824		}
825		provider, ok := cfg.(interface {
826			Provider(name string) (ServiceProviderEntry, bool)
827		})
828		if !ok {
829			return nil
830		}
831		out := make(map[string]string)
832		for _, name := range names {
833			pcfg, ok := provider.Provider(name)
834			if !ok {
835				continue
836			}
837			location, missing := credentialMissingForProvider(name, pcfg)
838			if missing {
839				out[name] = location
840			}
841		}
842		if len(out) == 0 {
843			return nil
844		}
845		return out
846	}
847	
848	// credentialMissingForProvider returns (location, true) when the named
849	// provider needs an API key but the configured value is empty or obviously
850	// malformed. location names the field the operator should set.
851	//
852	// The malformed heuristic is intentionally narrow: it catches blatant typos
853	// (wrong prefix or length far below any plausible key) without ever calling
854	// the provider. Server-side validity is out of scope here — the rejection
855	// path will materialize the credential_invalid reason separately.
856	func credentialMissingForProvider(name string, pcfg ServiceProviderEntry) (string, bool) {
857		if normalizeServiceProviderType(pcfg.Type) != "openrouter" {
858			return "", false
859		}
860		key := strings.TrimSpace(pcfg.APIKey)
861		location := fmt.Sprintf("providers.%s.api_key (or OPENROUTER_API_KEY env)", name)
862		if key == "" {
863			return location, true
864		}
865		if !openrouterAPIKeyWellFormed(key) {
866			return location, true
867		}
868		return "", false
869	}
870	
871	// openrouterAPIKeyWellFormed reports whether s plausibly resembles a real
872	// OpenRouter API key. The heuristic checks the well-known "sk-or-" prefix and
873	// a minimum length floor that excludes obvious typos. It does NOT confirm
874	// server-side validity.
875	func openrouterAPIKeyWellFormed(s string) bool {
876		const (
877			expectedPrefix  = "sk-or-"
878			minPlausibleLen = 20
879		)
880		if !strings.HasPrefix(s, expectedPrefix) {
881			return false
882		}
883		if len(s) < minPlausibleLen {
884			return false
885		}
886		return true
887	}
888	
889	// isSnapshotDialFailure preserved as a back-compat alias for the v0.13.0

1470						candidateReason = fmt.Sprintf("provider %s known unreachable (last dial failure %s ago)", candidateProviderIdentity(h, p), in.Now.Sub(failedAt).Truncate(time.Second))
1471						filterReason = FilterReasonUnhealthy
1472					}
1473				}
1474	
1475				// Proactive probe gate: endpoints known unreachable from background/startup
1476				// probing are hard-gated. An explicit provider pin bypasses the gate so
1477				// operators can still force a dead-endpoint route.
1478				if eligible && p.Name != "" && req.Provider != candidateProviderIdentity(h, p) {
1479					if _, ok := in.ProbeUnreachable[p.Name]; ok {
1480						eligible = false
1481						candidateReason = fmt.Sprintf("provider %s endpoint unreachable (aliveness probe failed)", candidateProviderIdentity(h, p))
1482						filterReason = FilterReasonEndpointUnreachable
1483					}
1484				}
1485	
1486				// Generic provider-eligibility override: the service layer can mark
1487				// any provider ineligible with a typed FilterReason and freshness
1488				// timestamp without baking provider-specific knowledge into the
1489				// engine. Mirrors the endpoint_unreachable gate's explicit-pin
1490				// bypass so operators can still force a route intentionally.
1491				if eligible && p.Name != "" && req.Provider != candidateProviderIdentity(h, p) {
1492					identity := candidateProviderIdentity(h, p)
1493					if ov, ok := lookupEligibilityOverride(in.ProviderEligibilityOverrides, identity); ok && ov.FilterReason != FilterReasonEligible {
1494						eligible = false
1495						candidateReason = formatEligibilityOverrideReason(identity, ov)
1496						filterReason = ov.FilterReason
1497					}
1498				}
1499	
1500				// Credential gate: providers that need an API key but lack one (or
1501				// hold an obviously malformed value) are disqualified before any
1502				// dispatch so the operator sees the root cause instead of a 401.
1503				// The gate runs even when the operator pins the provider — there is
1504				// no fallback path that could possibly authenticate.
1505				if eligible && len(in.ProviderCredentialMissing) > 0 {
1506					identity := candidateProviderIdentity(h, p)
1507					if location, ok := lookupCredentialMissing(in.ProviderCredentialMissing, identity); ok {
1508						eligible = false
1509						candidateReason = fmt.Sprintf("provider %s credential missing (%s)", identity, location)
1510						filterReason = FilterReasonCredentialMissing
1511					}
1512				}
1513	
1514				// Credit-balance gate: providers whose cached account balance fell
1515				// below the configured threshold are disqualified before dispatch
1516				// so the operator sees the root cause instead of an
1517				// insufficient-credit response. The probe runs in the service
1518				// layer's freshness cache; the engine reads only the projected
1519				// evidence map, so the gate is O(1) and side-effect free.
1520				if eligible && len(in.ProviderCreditExhausted) > 0 {
1521					identity := candidateProviderIdentity(h, p)
1522					if ev, ok := lookupCreditExhausted(in.ProviderCreditExhausted, identity); ok {
1523						eligible = false
1524						candidateReason = fmt.Sprintf(
1525							"provider %s credit exhausted (balance $%.4f below threshold $%.2f, observed %s)",
1526							identity,
1527							ev.BalanceUSD,
1528							ev.ThresholdUSD,
1529							ev.ObservedAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),

1944	// openrouterCredentialGateService wires a service whose only configured
1945	// provider is openrouter pointing at an httptest server. The server's hit
1946	// counter must stay 0 through every credential-gate test: the gate is
1947	// synchronous and must not trigger discovery or dispatch.
1948	//
1949	// A fresh discovery cache fixture pre-seeds the snapshot path so background
1950	// /v1/models refresh has nothing to do — the only HTTP I/O an unfiltered
1951	// openrouter candidate could trigger is dispatch, which the credential gate
1952	// must block.
1953	func openrouterCredentialGateService(t *testing.T, apiKey string) (*service, *atomic.Int32) {
1954		t.Helper()
1955		var hits atomic.Int32
1956		srv := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, _ *http.Request) {
1957			hits.Add(1)
1958			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusTeapot)
1959		}))
1960		t.Cleanup(srv.Close)
1961	
1962		cacheDir := t.TempDir()
1963		t.Setenv("FIZEAU_CACHE_DIR", cacheDir)
1964		t.Setenv("PATH", "")
1965		cache := &discoverycache.Cache{Root: cacheDir}
1966		writeSnapshotDiscoveryFixture(
1967			t,
1968			cache,
1969			testDiscoverySourceName("openrouter", "openrouter", srv.URL+"/v1", ""),
1970			time.Now().UTC(),
1971			[]string{"openrouter/test-model"},
1972		)
1973	
1974		t.Cleanup(replaceRoutingCatalogForTest(t, openrouterCredentialGateCatalog(t)))
1975	
1976		sc := &fakeServiceConfig{
1977			providers: map[string]ServiceProviderEntry{
1978				"openrouter": {
1979					Type:    "openrouter",
1980					BaseURL: srv.URL + "/v1",
1981					APIKey:  apiKey,
1982					Model:   "openrouter/test-model",
1983				},
1984			},
1985			names:       []string{"openrouter"},
1986			defaultName: "openrouter",
1987		}
1988		svc := newTestService(t, ServiceOptions{
1989			ServiceConfig:       sc,
1990			QuotaRefreshContext: canceledRefreshContext(),
1991		})
1992		return svc, &hits
1993	}
1994	
1995	// installPanickingOpenRouterTransport replaces http.DefaultClient.Transport
1996	// with a roundtripper that panics on any request directed at the openrouter
1997	// host. Routing should never call out before the credential gate fires; if
1998	// it does, the panic fails the test immediately rather than masking the
1999	// regression behind a recovered error.
2000	func installPanickingOpenRouterTransport(t *testing.T, host string) {
2001		t.Helper()
2002		prev := http.DefaultClient.Transport
2003		http.DefaultClient.Transport = &openrouterPanicTransport{t: t, host: host, base: prev}
2004		t.Cleanup(func() { http.DefaultClient.Transport = prev })
2005	}
2006	
2007	type openrouterPanicTransport struct {
2008		t    *testing.T
2009		host string
2010		base http.RoundTripper
2011	}
2012	
2013	func (p *openrouterPanicTransport) RoundTrip(req *http.Request) (*http.Response, error) {
2014		if req != nil && req.URL != nil && p.host != "" && req.URL.Host == p.host {
2015			p.t.Fatalf("unexpected HTTP call to openrouter host %s while credential gate should have filtered the candidate: %s %s", p.host, req.Method, req.URL)
2016		}
2017		base := p.base
2018		if base == nil {
2019			base = http.DefaultTransport
2020		}
2021		return base.RoundTrip(req)
2022	}
2023

718				entry.QuotaOK = qs.OK

720				entry.SubscriptionOK = qs.OK
721				entry.QuotaPercentUsed = qs.PercentUsed
722				entry.QuotaTrend = qs.Trend

726			// Native "fiz" harness: enumerate snapshot-derived provider rows.
727			if name == "fiz" && s.opts.ServiceConfig != nil {
728				entry.Providers = s.snapshotProviderEntries(ctx, cat, snapshot)
729				// Tool support for the agent harness is per-(provider, model);
730				// the harness-level baseline is whether ANY provider supports
731				// tools. Engine OR-combines harness and provider SupportsTools
732				// so this lets a per-model no_tools catalog flag actually fire
733				// the RequiresTools gate when every provider's resolved model
734				// is no-tools.
735				if len(entry.Providers) > 0 {
736					entry.SupportsTools = anyProviderSupportsTools(entry.Providers)
737				} else {
738					entry.Available = false
739				}
740			}
741			// Subscription harnesses (claude/codex/gemini) expose multiple tiers
742			// under one auth. Use the catalog surface to enumerate the active
743			// concrete tier IDs so the engine emits one candidate per tier and
744			// the cost-aware scorer can pick the cheapest viable tier instead of
745			// silently collapsing to DefaultModel.
746			if entry.IsSubscription {
747				if tiers := catalogTierModelsForHarnessSurface(cat, entry.Surface); len(tiers) > 0 {
748					entry.AutoRoutingModels = tiers
749					entry.SupportedModels = appendUniqueModelIDs(entry.SupportedModels, tiers...)
750				}
751			}
752			s.applySubscriptionRoutingCost(&entry, cat)
753			entries = append(entries, entry)
